raw_text_excerpt: From the Pricing page (platform.claude.com/docs/en/docs/about-claude/pricing) model-pricing table verbatim: 'Claude Opus 4.7 | $5 / MTok | $6.25 / MTok | $10 / MTok | $0.50 / MTok | $25 / MTok'; 'Claude Sonnet 4.6 | $3 / MTok | $3.75 / MTok | $6 / MTok | $0.30 / MTok | $15 / MTok'; 'Claude Haiku 4.5 | $1 / MTok | $1.25 / MTok | $2 / MTok | $0.10 / MTok | $5 / MTok'. Columns: Base Input Tokens | 5m Cache Writes | 1h Cache Writes | Cache Hits & Refreshes | Output Tokens. Corroborated on the Models Overview page (platform.claude.com/docs/en/docs/about-claude/models/overview) latest-models-comparison Pricing row: 'Claude Opus 4.7: $5 / input MTok, $25 / output MTok'; 'Claude Sonnet 4.6: $3 / input MTok, $15 / output MTok'; 'Claude Haiku 4.5: $1 / input MTok, $5 / output MTok'. Legacy-models table shows Claude Opus 4.1 + Claude Opus 4 (deprecated) at $15 / input MTok and $75 / output MTok, consistent with the M10 attestation epoch.
notes: Pricing attestation archived at M11a-extension Commit A per pre-reg ~/.claude/plans/m11a-extension-v4-prompt-n20.md §D11 HARD GATE + D14-H9 cross-milestone alias drift defense. Three findings: (1) Claude Opus 4.7 observed-published rate $5/$25 — BYTEWISE IDENTICAL to M11b attestation runs/data/20a-pricing-attestation-2026-05-13.json (no rate rotation in the 2-day interval). Per pre-reg §D11 + D14 hardening: M10 lock ($15 input / $75 output) HOLDS for all M11a-extension internal cost modeling to preserve cross-milestone consistency with M10/M10b/M11a/M11b cost numbers in runs/17,18,19,20. agent/arbiter.py OPUS_INPUT_USD_PER_M = 15.0 + OPUS_OUTPUT_USD_PER_M = 75.0 remain unchanged through Commit B (V4 wiring touches the prompt constant + routing only). (2) Claude Sonnet 4.6 rate $3 / $15 — BYTEWISE IDENTICAL to M11b attestation; matches agent/arbiter.py SONNET_INPUT_USD_PER_M = 3.0 + SONNET_OUTPUT_USD_PER_M = 15.0; no plan §D10 update needed. (3) Claude Haiku 4.5 rate $1 / $5 — BYTEWISE IDENTICAL to M11b attestation; matches agent/arbiter.py HAIKU_INPUT_USD_PER_M = 1.0 + HAIKU_OUTPUT_USD_PER_M = 5.0; no plan §D10 update needed. Zero observed-published-rate drift between M11b Commit A (2026-05-13) and M11a-extension Commit A (2026-05-15). Cross-tier Pareto ratios reported in this milestone (V2-Opus vs V4-Opus matched-arbiter; V4-Opus vs V4-Sonnet vs V4-Haiku cross-tier) are insensitive to a uniform Opus-rate scaling factor — re-pricing Opus to $5/$25 would scale all Opus-tier absolute $/hit numbers by 1/3 but leave matched-arbiter and cross-tier ratios unchanged at the per-trace level. Absolute Opus-tier $/hit numbers in M11a-extension's paper appendix are reported at the M10-locked rate; the paper appendix reproduces the M11b caveat verbatim that deployment-cost-projection should divide Opus-tier absolute numbers by ~3 for current-Anthropic-rate-deployment alignment. M11a-extension is the FINAL empirical milestone before paper drafting; preserving M10 lock through paper-line cost numbers maintains internal consistency through the complete M8b → M10 → M10b → M11a → M11b → M11a-extension milestone series. The D14-H9 Surface #13 cross-milestone Claude alias drift defense rests on (a) this attestation showing zero published-rate drift, and (b) Commit B's belt-and-suspenders bit-identical V2-Opus + V2-Sonnet + V2-Haiku re-run against M11b's runs/data/20*-content-{opus,sonnet,haiku}-v2-*.json (PASS gate for cross-milestone alias-dispatch stability).
